So welcome to section 13, which is going to be our first close, the upgraded FAQ. So this is a new spin on an old classic. And instead of this half-azardly throwing a bunch of questions, hoping something sticks, we're intentionally creating a mini microcosm of a sales page within it. We're asking it to do more. We're asking it to coach decisiveness. So the upgraded FAQ coaches decisiveness on a more mental intellectual level while reassuring your reader that asking those questions is actually aligned with being a buyer, not an objection in the classical sense. And when done right, your upgraded FAQ has the power to counter objections, position you against competitors, reinforce the big insights and reframes you laid out in the first half of your sales page, create safety by prepaving the outcome, and help your prospect identify as a buyer all without breaking form. So each section of the upgraded FAQ does in fact begin with an objection in mind. But unlike more traditional FAQs, we welcome those objections. Like a good coach, we see those objections not as an obstacle to the conversion, but as an opportunity to educate, illuminate, and re-empower our clients to make powerful decisions. So how do we do that? How do we educate, illuminate, and empower our clients to make that decision? Well, here is a general theme plate. So we start off with a headline that frames questions or objections as a precursor to investing in your product. Question one will often take their biggest objection and transform it into their biggest opportunity. Question two will take a major fear or objection and use it to point to a specific and unique aspect of your product that answers it. The third will bust a major industry wide myth, aka sacred cow, that in turn empowers them to take action. The fourth can position against competitors or alternatives and get buy-in on your unique and specific way of doing things. Number five has a springboard into your fuller USP. And six, I like to answer the time objection or any other common one that might be used in your industry. And then you could springboard into the offer and optional, but answer any further questions about your guarantee or return policy. Now a little editing note here. When reviewing your work in this section, read your answers out loud by starting each one with, I'm so happy you asked that. And we do this to make sure you're not wielding objections in any kind of defensive way, but as an opportunity to enlighten your prospect and empower them to make the decision. If you need inspiration and want to see how this plays out in real time, I invite you to watch the example breakdowns in the next video. Or if you are ready to take a stab at this, take some time now and write your upgraded FAQ and I will see you in the next section.
